name: BrainsWay Ltd. - International Headquarters
description_long: BrainsWay is the pioneering developer of Deep TMS technology, headquartered in Jerusalem. Founded in 2003 as the exclusive licensee of the NIH-owned patent for the H-coil Deep TMS system, BrainsWay has expanded its presence across Israel with 11 system installations. Their Deep TMS technology is FDA-cleared for major depressive disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, and smoking addiction. The company collaborates with leading Israeli hospitals and has driven a significant expansion of TMS access following a 45% increase in reimbursement rates. In 2024, the Israel Ministry of Defense approved reimbursement of Deep TMS therapy for qualifying patients with PTSD at Israeli public hospitals.

doctors_data.name: Dr. Abraham Zangen
doctors_data.title: Co-Inventor, Deep TMS H-Coil
doctors_data.university: National Institutes of Health (NIH) / Ben-Gurion University of the Negev
doctors_data.bio: Co-inventor of the Deep TMS H-coil technology while working at the NIH in the late 1990s. Professor of Neuroscience at Ben-Gurion University, with decades of research in brain stimulation and neuromodulation.

name: Tel Aviv Sourasky Medical Center - Psychiatry Division
description_long: Tel Aviv Sourasky Medical Center (Ichilov) is a 1,500-bed world-class governmental academic medical center serving over one million people and recognized as Israel's leading multidisciplinary healthcare institution. The Psychiatry Division offers biological treatments including medications, electroconvulsive therapy, and deep transcranial magnetic stimulation (Deep TMS). The center has been involved in significant TMS research including studies on ADHD, PTSD, and depression, and has participated in randomized controlled trials assessing the efficacy of Deep TMS for adults with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder.

name: Shalvata Mental Health Center
description_long: Shalvata Mental Health Center, located in Hod HaSharon, Israel, is a comprehensive psychiatric facility founded in 1956 by Clalit, Israel's largest health organization. Set in a pastoral environment, the center provides holistic mental health care using an integrative approach combining biological, psychological, and rehabilitative therapies. Shalvata uses transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) extensively in its clinical work and research. The center is affiliated with the Sackler School of Medicine at Tel Aviv University, enabling education and training for mental health professionals. The facility serves children, adolescents, adults, and seniors.

name: Beer Yaakov-Ness Ziona Mental Health Center
description_long: Beer Yaakov-Ness Ziona Mental Health Center is a major psychiatric facility in central Israel affiliated with Tel Aviv University. The center has been a key site for deep TMS clinical research, participating in prospective multicenter randomized controlled trials examining the efficacy and safety of deep transcranial magnetic stimulation for major depression. The center has also conducted double-blind studies using deep TMS for treating auditory hallucinations in schizophrenic patients. The facility offers inpatient and outpatient services for adults, children, adolescents, and older adults with various mental health disorders, as well as treatment programs for substance abuse and addiction.

name: Abarbanel Mental Health Center
description_long: Abarbanel Medical Center for Mental Health is one of Israel's leading psychiatric hospitals, established in 1944 in Bat Yam. The center is a BrainsWay Deep TMS provider, offering advanced neuromodulation treatment for depression and other psychiatric disorders. Abarbanel provides comprehensive inpatient and outpatient services for both children and adults, with specialized facilities for addiction treatment and suicide prevention. Emergency psychiatric care is available around the clock. The facility has recently been integrated administratively with Tel Aviv Sourasky Medical Center (Ichilov).

name: Geha Mental Health Center
description_long: Geha Mental Health Center, opened in 1942 in Petah Tikva, is a well-regarded psychiatric institution that is part of the Clalit Health Services network. The center operates a dedicated TMS department offering Deep TMS (Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation) treatment for depression, OCD, and smoking addiction. Geha provides a broad spectrum of medical services employing progressive diagnostic and treatment techniques for mental health disorders, rehabilitation, and social reintegration. The center serves patients across the lifespan from children to older adults, utilizing both traditional and innovative treatment methods.

name: Lev Hasharon Mental Health Center
description_long: Lev Hasharon Mental Health Center in Netanya operates across three campuses: a main campus with 263 inpatient beds, two clinics, and four day-treatment programs; a community campus in Netanya with three clinics and two day-treatment programs; and a campus for community housing for Holocaust survivors with 100 beds. The hospital is a designated BrainsWay Deep TMS provider and specializes in sexual trauma, addiction, psychogeriatrics, resistant depression with advanced technologies, and forensic psychiatry. Deep TMS at Lev Hasharon is FDA-approved for treatment of Depression (MDD), OCD, and Smoking Addiction.

name: Matzpen Mental Health Center
description_long: Matzpen Clinic is a private psychotherapy, psychiatry, and neurology centre founded in 2006 on the Tel Aviv seafront. The location offers panoramic views of the Mediterranean beach. As a highly specialised private clinic, Matzpen takes a comprehensive approach to outpatient diagnosis and treatment of psychiatric, neurological, psychosomatic, and addiction disorders. The center has international status and accepts citizens from different countries, with all clinic staff fluent in several languages. Matzpen's specialists have developed holistic and individualized treatment approaches, including neuromodulation therapies.

name: Sheba Medical Center - Psychiatry Division
description_long: Sheba Medical Center at Tel HaShomer is the most comprehensive medical center in the Middle East, featuring 159 medical departments and clinics and treating over one million patients annually. The Psychiatry Division has been actively involved in TMS research, with studies treating patients with major depression using transcranial magnetic stimulation showing significant response rates, particularly among non-psychotic patients. Sheba is home to the Israel National Center for Health Policy and Epidemiology Research, the Israel National Center for Medical Simulation, and numerous other research centers.

name: Hadassah Medical Center - Department of Psychiatry
description_long: Hadassah Medical Center at Ein Kerem in Jerusalem is one of Israel's most prestigious academic medical centers. The Department of Psychiatry at Hadassah-Hebrew University Medical Center has been involved in clinical research with deep TMS, including studies on post-traumatic stress disorder. The hospital has a long relationship with BrainsWay Deep TMS technology, and its physicians have participated in multi-site clinical trials evaluating Deep TMS combined with brief exposure therapy for PTSD. Hadassah serves as a major teaching hospital affiliated with the Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

name: Psykiatri Sydvast - Enheten for Hjarnstimulering
description_long: The Unit for Brain Stimulation (Enheten for Hjarnstimulering) at Psykiatri Sydvast offers both ECT and TMS treatment for patients with affective disorders. Located at Huddinge Hospital (part of Karolinska University Hospital), the clinic serves adult psychiatry patients across the southern Stockholm catchment area. The unit collaborates with Karolinska Institute as well as other healthcare providers, colleges, and universities. TMS treatment at this facility is offered as a standard treatment option for moderate to severe depression, in line with the Swedish National Board of Health and Welfare's 2017 guidelines.

name: Norra Stockholms Psykiatri - Mottagning for Hjarnstimulering
description_long: The Brain Stimulation Clinic (Mottagning for Hjarnstimulering) at Norra Stockholms Psykiatri, located in the Sankt Gorans hospital area in Stadshagen, Stockholm, primarily treats patients with depression using electroconvulsive treatment and repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS). The clinic has reported that rTMS alleviates depressive symptoms in every other patient treated. Norra Stockholms Psykiatri provides specialist psychiatric care based on regional care programs and is one of the major psychiatric providers in the Stockholm region.

name: Psykiatri Nordvast - Enheten for Hjarnstimulering
description_long: The Brain Stimulation Unit (Enheten for Hjarnstimulering) at Psykiatri Nordvast is a specialist unit at Danderyds Hospital offering ECT and rTMS treatment for patients with affective disorders. The unit serves the northwest Stockholm catchment area and provides evidence-based brain stimulation therapies for depression and other mood disorders. Treatment is offered in accordance with Swedish national guidelines recommending rTMS for moderate to severe depression as an alternative or complement to antidepressant medication.

name: Akademiska Sjukhuset - Mottagningen for Hjarnstimulering
description_long: The Brain Stimulation Unit (Mottagningen for Hjarnstimulering) at Akademiska Sjukhuset (Uppsala University Hospital) offers ECT and rTMS (repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation) as well as other psychiatric treatments requiring advanced monitoring. The unit is part of the section for Acute and Consultation Psychiatry. Uppsala University Hospital has been at the forefront of TMS research in Sweden, with studies demonstrating that magnetic stimulation of the brain can be an effective treatment for depression, including during pregnancy. The hospital was also the first in Sweden to offer transcranial ultrasound for brain disorders.

name: Sahlgrenska Universitetssjukhuset - Psykiatri
description_long: Sahlgrenska University Hospital is the largest hospital in Sweden with 17,000 employees, formed in 1997 by the merger of Sahlgrenska Hospital, Ostra Hospital, and Molndal Hospital. The hospital network, associated with the Sahlgrenska Academy at the University of Gothenburg, provides rTMS treatment through its psychiatric services. The psychiatric emergency department and adult psychiatric services are located at Ostra Hospital. Sahlgrenska has 2,000 beds across its three campuses and is a major center for psychiatric research and treatment in western Sweden.

name: rTMS Behandling
description_long: rTMS Behandling is a dedicated private TMS treatment provider in Sweden offering repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation for depression. The clinic provides rTMS as an effective alternative to antidepressant medication, with the treatment performed while the patient is awake and without negative effects on memory. Based on over 40 independent clinical studies involving more than 2,000 patients, rTMS has been demonstrated to be effective in treating severe depression. The clinic follows the Swedish National Board of Health and Welfare guidelines recommending rTMS for moderate to severe depression.

name: Capio Psykiatri Helsingborg
description_long: Capio Psykiatri Helsingborg is a private psychiatric clinic contracted with Region Skane, offering specialist psychiatric care for patients aged 18 and over. The clinic provides repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) as part of its psychiatric treatment offerings. Capio Hjarnhalsan, the mental health division of the Capio/Ramsay Sante healthcare group, opened its Helsingborg clinic at a central location and holds agreements with the regional health authority to provide psychiatric services including rTMS for treatment-resistant depression.

name: Connectomix - Netcare Milpark Hospital
description_long: Connectomix is a state-of-the-art TMS clinic group founded in 2019 and based in Johannesburg and Pretoria, backed by Netcare, the largest private hospital group in South Africa. The Milpark location is situated within the esteemed Netcare Milpark Hospital in Parktown, Johannesburg. Headed by Dr. Christos Profyris, a neurosurgeon who completed pre-clinical studies at Cambridge with a Double First Class and his medical degree with Distinction at Oxford, Connectomix employs a team of doctors, psychologists, and experts with a Neuroscientist overseeing treatment accuracy. The clinic treats depression, anxiety, PTSD, tinnitus, and other conditions using advanced connectome-based TMS protocols.

doctors_data.name: Dr. Christos Profyris
doctors_data.title: Founder & Neurosurgeon
doctors_data.university: University of Cambridge / University of Oxford / University of the Witwatersrand
doctors_data.bio: Dr. Christos Profyris completed pre-clinical medical studies at the University of Cambridge with a Double First Class and his medical degree with Distinction at the University of Oxford. He completed neurosurgical training at the University of Witwatersrand, holds a Fellowship from the Colleges of Medicine of South Africa and a Master of Medicine in Neurosurgery from Wits. He completed the Charles Teo Minimally Invasive Neurosurgery Fellowship in Sydney, Australia, and has published over 20 peer-reviewed publications with an active research interest in connectome-based TMS treatment.

name: Connectomix - Menlyn Maine
description_long: Connectomix Menlyn Maine is located in the Menlyn Maine precinct in Pretoria, easily accessible from surrounding suburbs and major roadways. As part of the Connectomix TMS clinic group backed by Netcare, the largest private hospital group in South Africa, this location offers advanced connectome-based TMS treatment for depression, anxiety, PTSD, tinnitus, and other neuropsychiatric conditions. The clinic employs extensively trained staff with a Neuroscientist overseeing treatment accuracy and continuous auditing of the navigation process.
address: Menlyn Maine Central Square, C/o Corobay & Aramist Avenue, Waterkloof Glen

doctors_data.name: Dr. Anrie Carstens
doctors_data.title: Psychiatrist
doctors_data.bio: Dr. Anrie Carstens has partnered with Connectomix founder Dr. Christos Profyris to bring TMS services to patients in the Gauteng region. She provides psychiatric consultation and TMS treatment oversight at the Pretoria location.

name: Rewire TMS Clinic - Claremont
description_long: Rewire TMS Clinic in Claremont is located within the Crescent Mediclinic in Cape Town's Southern Suburbs. Founded in 2021 by Dr. Marcelle Stastny, a psychiatrist with over twenty years of experience, Rewire offers 1Hz, 10Hz, and theta burst (modified SAINT) TMS treatments. The clinic's technicians obtained their TMS accreditation from Maastricht University. TMS treatment at Rewire is approved by the FDA, CE, and SAHPRA, and for treatment-resistant depression is covered by some South African medical aids, subject to authorisation. The clinic also serves foreign clients seeking TMS treatment combined with travel to Cape Town.
machines: TMS System (1Hz, 10Hz, Theta Burst / Modified SAINT protocols)

doctors_data.name: Dr. Marcelle Stastny
doctors_data.title: Founder & Psychiatrist
doctors_data.bio: Dr. Marcelle Stastny is a psychiatrist with over twenty years of experience who founded Rewire TMS Clinics in 2021. She holds an MBChB from the University of Pretoria and previously worked as a psychiatrist for the NHS in Exeter, UK. She has established Rewire branches across South Africa to improve access to TMS therapy.

name: Rewire TMS Clinic - N1 City (Claro Clinic)
description_long: Rewire TMS Clinic at N1 City is located within the Life Path Health Claro Clinic in Cape Town's Northern Suburbs. As part of the Rewire network founded by Dr. Marcelle Stastny, this location offers 1Hz, 10Hz, and theta burst (modified SAINT) TMS treatments administered by accredited technicians trained at Maastricht University. Life Path Health Claro Clinic is a private psychiatric hospital contracted with major South African medical aids, providing a supportive clinical environment for TMS treatment.
address: Claro Clinic, Syfred Douglas Street, N1 City
machines: TMS System (1Hz, 10Hz, Theta Burst / Modified SAINT protocols)

doctors_data.name: Dr. Marcelle Stastny
doctors_data.title: Founder & Psychiatrist
doctors_data.bio: Dr. Marcelle Stastny oversees the Rewire TMS network including this N1 City location. She holds an MBChB from the University of Pretoria and has over twenty years of psychiatric experience.

name: Rewire TMS Clinic - Roodepoort
description_long: Rewire TMS Clinic in Roodepoort serves the Gauteng region from its location at Cat's Corner in Weltevreden Park. As part of the Rewire TMS network, this Johannesburg-area clinic offers the same advanced TMS protocols available at the Cape Town locations, including 1Hz, 10Hz, and theta burst (modified SAINT) treatments. The clinic extends Rewire's reach to patients in Gauteng who require non-invasive brain stimulation therapy for treatment-resistant depression and other psychiatric conditions.
address: Cat's Corner, 39 Kroton Way, Weltevreden Park
machines: TMS System (1Hz, 10Hz, Theta Burst / Modified SAINT protocols)

name: Dr. Kobus van der Walt - TMS Practice
description_long: Dr. Kobus van der Walt operates a private psychiatric practice in Somerset West, Cape Town, with a particular focus on interventional psychiatry. Since 2019 in full-time private practice, the clinic offers advanced neuromodulation therapies including TMS (Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation), IV Ketamine (IVK), transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS), and transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation (tVNS). TMS is used as a non-invasive treatment using focused magnetic pulses to stimulate brain areas associated with mood regulation, most commonly for treatment-resistant depression. The practice serves patients across Stellenbosch, the Winelands, Cape Town Metro, Overberg, and the broader Western Cape.
address: Suite 105, Halo Medical Centre, 27 Gardner Williams Avenue, Paardevlei

doctors_data.name: Dr. Kobus van der Walt
doctors_data.title: Specialist Psychiatrist
doctors_data.bio: Dr. Kobus van der Walt is a qualified specialist psychiatrist based in Somerset West with a particular interest in interventional psychiatry. Since 2019 in full-time private practice, he offers personalised, evidence-informed psychiatric care to adults and adolescents, with a focus on individuals navigating persistent, complex, or treatment-resistant mental health difficulties. His practice offers TMS, IV Ketamine, tDCS, tVNS, and home-based sleep studies.

name: Careline Clinic - TMS Unit
description_long: Careline Clinic in Kimberley, Northern Cape, has procured a Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS) machine to treat major depressive disorders, anxiety, and treatment-resistant depressive disorders. The clinic provides Substance Abuse Psychiatry, Adult Voluntary Psychiatry, and Child & Adolescent Psychiatry services. Located on Provincial Road R31 in El Toro Park, the clinic offers comprehensive mental and physical wellbeing services including inpatient psychiatric care.
address: R31 Provincial Road, El Toro Park

doctors_data.name: Dr. Netsa Kirimi
doctors_data.title: Psychiatrist
doctors_data.bio: Dr. Netsa Kirimi provides psychiatric services at Careline Clinic in Kimberley, including oversight of the TMS treatment program for depression and anxiety disorders.

name: Connectomix - TMS Safari (Cape Town)
description_long: Connectomix TMS Safari is a unique program combining intensive TMS treatment with a South African travel experience. Based on the success of their Johannesburg and Pretoria clinics, Connectomix has expanded to Cape Town offering international patients the opportunity to undergo a full course of TMS therapy while experiencing South Africa. The program treats depression with FDA, CE, and SAHPRA-approved TMS protocols. Patients receive comprehensive connectome-based TMS treatment under the oversight of trained neuroscientists and the clinical team led by Dr. Christos Profyris.
address: Cape Town (location details available upon booking)

doctors_data.name: Dr. Christos Profyris
doctors_data.title: Founder & Neurosurgeon
doctors_data.university: University of Cambridge / University of Oxford / University of the Witwatersrand
doctors_data.bio: Dr. Christos Profyris oversees the TMS Safari program, bringing his expertise in connectome-based TMS treatment to international patients visiting South Africa.
